reasoning: Based on current reporting as of late February and early March 2025, Matt Gaetz is the only Trump Cabinet nominee (among the 15 executive departments and specifically cited Cabinet-level roles) who has withdrawn or been voted down. Controversial nominees such as Pete Hegseth, Tulsi Gabbard, and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. have all secured Senate confirmation. Chad Chronister withdrew from consideration for the DEA Administrator role, but the DEA is not listed in the market's specific criteria for Cabinet-level positions. With the vast majority of Cabinet positions now formally confirmed and confirmed individuals shielded from being 'withdrawn or voted down', it is mathematically nearly impossible to reach the required threshold of three failed nominations before the March 31, 2025 deadline. The market was trading around 3% highly reflecting this reality. Given the strict constraints on the minimum forecasting probability bounds, a 5% estimate is highly confident that the outcome will resolve to NO.
